file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Now I'll add the `hash_sha256` type following the existing pattern.

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

124	        def validate(input_value):
125	            return self.validate(input_value)
126

129	    def __get_pydantic_core_schema__(
130	        self, source_type: Any, handler: GetCoreSchemaHandler
131	    ) -> cs.CoreSchema:
132	        return cs.no_info_plain_validator_function(lambda value: self.validate(value))
133	
134	    def __get_pydantic_json_schema__(
135	        self, core_schema: cs.CoreSchema, handler: GetJsonSchemaHandler
136	    ) -> JsonSchemaValue:
137	        return {"type": "string", "pattern": to_regex(self)}
138	
139	    def validate(self, value: str) -> str:
140	        pattern = to_regex(self)
141	        compiled = re.compile(pattern)
142	        if not compiled.fullmatch(str(value)):
143	            raise ValueError(
144	                f"Input should be in the language of the regular expression {pattern}"
145	            )
146	        return value
147	
148	    def matches(self, value: str) -> bool:
149	        """Check that a given value is in the language defined by the Term.
150	
151	        We make the assumption that the language defined by the term can
152	        be defined with a regular expression.
153	
154	        """
155	        pattern = to_regex(self)
156	        compiled = re.compile(pattern)
157	        if compiled.fullmatch(str(value)):
158	            return True
159	        return False

162	        """Display the regex tree in ASCII format."""
163	        branch = "└── " if is_last else "├── "
164	        result = indent + branch + self._display_node() + "\n"
165	
166	        # Calculate the new indent for children
167	        new_indent = indent + ("    " if is_last else "│   ")
168	
169	        # Let each subclass handle its children

content.text: Good, `fullmatch` is used so the 64-char hex pattern is correctly anchored. Now let's verify it works and generate the patch.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b8'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'))
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))
print(hash_sha256.matches('g3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'))
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))
"

python -c "
import hashlib
h = hashlib.sha256(b'').hexdigest()
print(h, len(h))
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
print(hash_sha256.matches(h))
print(hash_sha256.matches(h.upper()))
"

content.text: Works correctly for actual 64-character hashes (the PR description's example string had a typo/extra char). Now let's check for coordination messages and finalize the patch.
Agent1 also edited the same file, adding an `ssn` type after `isbn`. Let me check the current file state for conflicts.

 )

content.text: Added a `hash_sha256` type in `outlines/types/__init__.py` as a `Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")`, matching the existing pattern used by `isbn`, `email`, etc. Verified it correctly validates real SHA-256 hashes (64 hex chars, case-insensitive) and rejects wrong-length or non-hex strings, and that existing tests in `tests/types/test_custom_types.py` still pass. Patch written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

result: Added a `hash_sha256` type in `outlines/types/__init__.py` as a `Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")`, matching the existing pattern used by `isbn`, `email`, etc. Verified it correctly validates real SHA-256 hashes (64 hex chars, case-insensitive) and rejects wrong-length or non-hex strings, and that existing tests in `tests/types/test_custom_types.py` still pass. Patch written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.
